fresh_facts.claim: The United States has maintained an active naval blockade of Iranian ports in the Persian Gulf since April 13, 2026, enforced by the USS Tripoli, guided-missile destroyers, and F-35B stealth fighters.
Over 3,500 sailors and Marines are conducting advanced boarding drills and VBSS tactics as part of the Persian Gulf blockade operations.
Iran placed its military on high alert in response to the US blockade.
Brigadier General Muhammad Acrimania cancelled traditional Army Day parades in favor of combat readiness exercises.
Iranian military command issued a 'shoot-on-sight directive' against any American personnel found on Iranian soil, nullifying the existing ceasefire for U.S. forces.
As of April 18, 2026, ships reported attacks in the Strait of Hormuz after Iran warned it would close the strait until the U.S. blockade is lifted.
A tanker and container ship reported attacks off the coast of Oman according to UK maritime officials.

A two-week ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran was set to expire on April 19, 2026, with the agreement stipulating Iran would open the Strait of Hormuz in exchange for a halt to fighting and bombardments.
The U.S. struck three Iranian nuclear facilities at Fordow, Natanz, and Isfahan using bunker buster bombs during Operation Midnight Hammer.
Iran rejected US talks 'under the shadow of threats,' warning of 'new cards' and military options if no agreement is reached before the ceasefire ends.
Trump stated negotiations will conclude with everyone being happy and extended the ceasefire but deemed further extension 'highly unlikely'.
A second round of talks between the U.S. and Iran was planned in Pakistan with JD Vance and US officials attending.
Iran cited US violations as hindering progress in negotiations.
US-Israeli strikes on February 28, 2026 killed Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and senior officials.

An Interim Leadership Council comprising President Masoud Pezeshkian, Chief Justice Gholam-Hossein Mohseni-Eje'i, and Guardian Council representative Alireza Arafi holds temporary power following the February 28 strikes.
The Assembly of Experts is selecting a successor to the Supreme Leader with no clear timeline or frontrunner, fueling Iran's most severe political crisis since 1979.
Divisions over US negotiations have strengthened hardline elements within Iranian leadership.
Iran's economy has sharply deteriorated 100 days after January 2026 protests and amid war, with millions feared unemployed.
No compensation has been provided for properties destroyed in strikes, sparking anger among Iranians.
The Supreme National Security Council approved selective global internet access for businesses via 'internet pro' to sustain economic activity.
Authorities are defacing or destroying graves of January 2026 protesters in Rasht and Tehran.
January 2026 events mark one of the most widespread and violent protest waves recently, with a deadly massacre on January 8-9.
Post-strike crackdown detained tens of thousands of people, with disputed casualties reported as approximately 3,000 deaths by Iran and much higher estimates by human rights groups.
The US embassy warned of new Iran-linked militia threats in Iraq and Kurdistan.
Trump demanded an end to Iran's nuclear program in talks, claiming a new deal would be better than the JCPOA.
Democrats criticized Trump's Iran policy as a 'boondoggle' without an exit strategy.
Oil prices dipped on peace hopes while gold remained steady above $4,800 amid ceasefire uncertainty.

regime_fall.current_status: The US-Iran ceasefire formally expired on April 19, 2026, with both sides conducting military escalations while diplomatic talks reconvene in Pakistan on April 21; a US naval blockade enforces economic strangulation as Iran's Interim Leadership Council struggles to navigate succession amid hardline pressure and severe internal instability.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Trump stated the ceasefire was extended but deemed further extension 'highly unlikely,' while JD Vance and US officials prepare for negotiations in Pakistan, even as Iran maintains a 'shoot-on-sight directive' against US personnel and the military conducts combat readiness exercises.
